query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "memlens: benchmarking multimodal long-term memory in large vision-lang" computer science
rationale: This paper satisfies criterion (d) as a foundational methodology and canonical reference for the domain of Large Vision-Language Model (LVLM) evaluation, which is the specific context of the user's "MemLens" follow-up. Additionally, it meets criterion (b) by establishing the empirical baseline for multimodal capabilities and hallucination issues in LVLMs, providing the necessary prior work against which a new "long-term memory" benchmark would be compared.
